name: MCP Filesystem
summary: Sandboxed filesystem access (list, read, blob, stat, duplicate detection and guarded write/move/rename) through fs:// URI commands.
description: MCP Filesystem is a urirun connector package that exposes sandboxed filesystem operations as fs:// routes. Everything resolves under a single root directory (IFURI_FS_ROOT); paths escaping the root are rejected. Binary blob reads are base64 and size-capped. Duplicate detection is exact SHA-256 matching. Guarded write/move commands default to dry-run and do not overwrite by default. Routes are declared with typed @handler functions, run in-process, return structured JSON, and can be projected to MCP tools / A2A skills.
useCases: Let a flow list and read project files without bespoke shell commands.
Bridge MCP filesystem semantics to URI-addressable routes for host workflows.
Inspect file metadata (size, mtime) from a URI flow with a sandbox boundary.
Find exact duplicate invoice attachments before grouping or archiving.
Let a stronger host fetch a sandboxed binary blob from a weaker node for local OCR/LLM processing.
Move confirmed non-invoice files into a no_invoice quarantine folder without leaving the sandbox.
Flatten nested invoice folders into month-level files with normalized names.
Prune empty directory scaffolding left after guarded moves.
Deliver small operator scripts such as index.php/run.sh into a sandboxed folder.
